patches.summary: Replace c.http.Jar swap in graphQLGet with a per-request Cookie header. Eliminates the data race on the shared client field flagged by Greptile on PR #740.
patches.reason: graphQLGet previously did `old := c.http.Jar; c.http.Jar = jar; defer ... = old`. With defaultClient as a package-global, any concurrent graphQLGet caller would race on the .Jar field. Race detector currently passes only because no test exercises concurrent paths. Building the Cookie header per-request and passing it via the existing headers map produces the identical wire result (Cookie: header on the outbound request) without shared mutable state. buildCookieHeader is a pure helper covered by three unit tests.
patches.validated_outcome: 162 tests / 21 packages green with -race (added TestBuildCookieHeader_Multiple, _Empty, _SkipsNilAndEmptyName). Live `airbnb-listing get` still returns total=$514 / per_night=$171.18.

patches.summary: Detect datadome and Akamai bot-defense responses; return a typed cliutil.BotChallengeError with a remediation hint. Previously, any 4xx other than 429 collapsed into a raw HTTP-status string with a 300-char body truncation, so a datadome challenge looked indistinguishable from a generic forbidden.
patches.reason: The CLI's source/airbnb path had no awareness of Airbnb's (or any other site's) bot-defense layer. A datadome 403 with a JSON {url: 'https://geo.captcha-delivery.com/captcha/...'} body would surface to the user as 'GET https://www.airbnb.com/api/v3/X returned HTTP 403: {url:\"https..."'. Adding a typed sentinel lets callers errors.As-route on it, lets the limiter halve its adaptive rate on bot challenges (treating them like 429 for ramp purposes), and lets us print a remediation hint pointing at 'auth login --chrome'. Detector matches three datadome signatures (Set-Cookie: datadome=, Server: dd-*, geo.captcha-delivery.com in body) and two Akamai signatures (<title>Bot or Not, captcha-pwa script reference), mirroring the existing VRBO detector pattern.
patches.validated_outcome: 9 new TestIsBotChallenge_* and TestBotChallengeError_* tests pass: datadome cookie/server-header/body-marker detection, Akamai title/captcha-pwa detection, generic-forbidden negative case, 200-OK negative case, nil-resp guard, errors.As routing. Full suite 157 tests / 21 packages green; headline `airbnb-listing get` still returns total=$514 / per_night=$171.18 (no regression).

patches.summary: Add 25% jitter to the 429 retry sleep; fall back to Backoff(attempt) when Retry-After header is absent so subsequent retries grow rather than re-sleeping the same 5s default.
patches.reason: The previous retry loop slept for cliutil.RetryAfter(resp), which returns 5s when the Retry-After header is missing or unparseable. Across attempts the wait never grew, and a fleet of retrying clients would synchronize their retries (thundering herd). Two changes: detect resp.Header.Get('Retry-After') == '' and use cliutil.Backoff(attempt) in that case; wrap the chosen base wait with a 0-to-base/4 jitter so retries spread out. Jitter helper guards against base < 4ns so rand.Int63n never panics on zero.
patches.validated_outcome: TestJitterBounds and TestJitterZeroBase pass (1000 samples per base, all in [0, base/4); zero-base returns 0 without panic). Full suite 148 tests / 21 packages green.

patches.summary: Add SetRate method on cliutil.AdaptiveLimiter; thread --rate-limit through source/airbnb scrape client; gate on cmd.Flags().Changed so default invocations keep the existing 0.5 rps baseline.
patches.reason: The --rate-limit flag was wired to internal/client/client.go (the generated REST client) but the actual airbnb traffic goes through internal/source/airbnb/client.go's package-global defaultClient with a hardcoded 0.5 rps limiter that never saw the flag. Users following SKILL.md's --rate-limit docs got no behavior change. SetRate as a method on AdaptiveLimiter (mutates rate/floor under existing mu, sets a disabled flag for rps<=0) preserves the limiter's adaptive-ramp history across rate changes; no pointer swap on defaultClient.limiter, so concurrent in-flight do() calls do not race. Gated on cmd.Flags().Changed('rate-limit') in PersistentPreRunE so default invocations stay non-regressive.
patches.validated_outcome: 7 new TestAdaptiveLimiter_SetRate* tests pass including race-detector. Full suite (146 tests / 21 packages) clean. Live: `airbnb-listing search ... --rate-limit 4.0` completes faster than default; `--rate-limit 0` disables waiting; unset flag preserves existing 0.5 rps baseline.

patches.summary: Rewrite BookingPrice variables to {id, dateRange, guestCounts, 4 includeFragment booleans}; refit priceBreakdownFromAny to read structuredDisplayPrice + explanationData.priceDetails. The persisted-query hash was NOT stale (F3 misdiagnosis); the schema validator was rejecting the variables shape.
patches.reason: Empirical capture via .manuscripts/20260502-210359/.../airbnb-capture.har showed the real request variables are {id, dateRange:{startDate,endDate}, guestCounts:{numberOfAdults}, 4 includeFragment booleans}. The CLI was sending a flat {checkin, checkout, adults} shape that tripped ValidationError. With all four includeFragment booleans set true, the response carries pdpPresentation.bookIt.structuredDisplayPrice with primaryLine.price (display total) and explanationData.priceDetails[].items[] (line items). priceBreakdownFromAny was looking for the legacy {label, amount} fee shape that this schema no longer carries; updated to parse the new shape with the legacy walker retained as fallback.
patches.validated_outcome: Live `airbnb-listing get 18413186 --checkin 2026-05-26 --checkout 2026-05-29 --adults 1` returns total=$514, subtotal=$513.53, per_night=$171.18, currency=USD. End-to-end pricing path restored.

patches.summary: Extract price, reviews, and location lines from the flat Airbnb SSR Apollo cache shape; legacy nested envelope kept as fallback.
patches.reason: Airbnb's current SSR ships each search-result entry as a flat listing card (`structuredDisplayPrice` at the top level, no `pricingQuote` envelope). The original parser looked for `pricingQuote.structuredStayDisplayPrice` and a nested `listing` object, so every search result returned null for price, per-night, and review-count fields. `reviewsCount` now ships null routinely; we backfill from `avgRatingLocalized` ("4.98 (576)") and `avgRatingA11yLabel`.
patches.validated_outcome: Live `airbnb-listing search Mercer-Island... --agent` now returns populated `primary_price`, `per_night_price`, and `reviews_count` on every result. Unit tests cover both flat (current) and nested (legacy) SSR shapes.

patches.summary: Send X-Airbnb-API-Key (and companion X-Airbnb-* headers) on every GraphQL request; scrape the key from homepage SSR with a constant fallback.
patches.reason: Airbnb's GraphQL gateway rejects unauthenticated requests with `{error:"invalid_key", error_code:400}`. The original `graphQLGet` sent only `Accept: application/json` plus the cookie jar, so `StaysPdpBookItQuery`, `WishlistIndexPageQuery`, and `WishlistItemsAsyncQuery` all 400'd. Restoring the public web API key fixes the headline blocker for `cheapest`, `plan`, `compare`, and `watch`.
patches.validated_outcome: Live `airbnb-listing get 18413186 --checkin ... --checkout ...` no longer returns the `invalid_key` error. The scraper falls back to the well-known constant `d306zoyjsyarp7ifhu67rjxn52tv0t20` if the homepage scrape fails (bot-block, network error).
